grants.title: NYSCA Reserve Fund Grant
grants.summary: Supports New York State-based nonprofit arts organizations in establishing or building reserve funds.
grants.description: The NYSCA Reserve Fund Grant is a program designed to help nonprofit arts organizations across New York State establish or enhance reserve funds. Administered by the New York Foundation for the Arts, the grant aims to provide financial stability and resilience to organizations with operating budgets under $3 million. Grants range from $10,000 to $75,000, based on the applicant's budget. Recipients also receive technical support and consultation for long-term sustainability. Eligible organizations must have received NYSCA funding in the last three years. Guidelines for the next round will be released in Spring 2026.
grants.eligibility_notes: Nonprofit arts organizations with operating budgets under $3 million that have received NYSCA funding in the last three years.
sdg_alignment: SDG 8: Decent Work and Economic Growth
key_requirements: Operating budget under $3 million
Received NYSCA funding in the last three years
Nonprofit arts organization based in New York State

grants.title: New York State Council on the Arts Grants
grants.summary: Funds arts and cultural projects across New York State.
grants.description: The New York State Council on the Arts (NYSCA) provides funding to support arts and cultural initiatives throughout New York State. Grants are available for organizations and individual artists in various disciplines, including dance, music, literature, visual arts, and more. Applicants can search for grants by year, program, county, and other criteria using the NYSCA grant search engine. The program aims to enhance access to the arts, foster creativity, and support economic development. Applications are submitted through the NYSCA website.
grants.eligibility_notes: Open to individual artists and organizations involved in arts and cultural projects within New York State.
key_requirements: Applicants must be based in New York State.
Projects must align with NYSCA's arts and cultural focus.
Applications must be submitted through the NYSCA grant search engine.

grants.title: NYSCA Reserve Fund Grant
grants.summary: Supports nonprofit arts organizations in New York State to establish or build reserve funds.
grants.description: The NYSCA Reserve Fund Grant is designed to help nonprofit arts organizations across New York State establish or strengthen reserve funds. The program aims to enhance financial stability, enabling organizations to weather challenges, cover unexpected expenses, and leverage new funding opportunities. Administered in partnership with the New York Foundation for the Arts (NYFA), the grant provides technical support and consultation for long-term sustainability. Eligible organizations must have received NYSCA funding in the last three years and have operating budgets under $3 million. Guidelines for the next round will be released in Spring 2026.
grants.eligibility_notes: Nonprofit arts organizations in New York State with operating budgets under $3 million that have received NYSCA funding in the last three years.
sdg_alignment: SDG 8: Decent Work and Economic Growth
key_requirements: Must be a nonprofit arts organization based in New York State.
Operating budget under $3 million.
Received NYSCA funding in the last three years.

grants.title: NYSCA Reserve Fund Grant
grants.summary: Supports nonprofit arts organizations in New York State to establish or build reserve funds.
grants.description: The NYSCA Reserve Fund Grant is designed to help New York State-based nonprofit arts organizations establish a reserve fund or build on an existing reserve fund. The program aims to strengthen financial stability, enable organizations to weather challenges, and support long-term sustainability. Eligible organizations must have received NYSCA funding in the last three years and have operating budgets under $3 million. Grants range from $10,000 to $75,000, based on the applicant's budget. Technical support and consultation are provided to grantees for strategic planning and capacity building. Guidelines for the next round will be released in Spring 2026.
grants.eligibility_notes: Nonprofit arts organizations in New York State with operating budgets under $3 million that have received NYSCA funding in the last three years.
sdg_alignment: SDG 8: Decent Work and Economic Growth
key_requirements: Must be a nonprofit arts organization based in New York State.
Operating budget under $3 million.
Received NYSCA funding in the last three years.

grants.title: NYSCA Reserve Fund Grant
grants.summary: This grant supports nonprofit arts organizations in New York State to establish or build reserve funds.
grants.description: The NYSCA Reserve Fund Grant is designed to help New York State-based nonprofit arts organizations establish a reserve fund or build on an existing reserve fund. The program aims to provide financial stability, enabling organizations to weather challenges, cover unexpected expenses, and strengthen their balance sheets. Grants range from $10,000 to $75,000, determined by the applicant's operating budget. Eligible organizations must have received NYSCA funding in the last three years and have operating budgets under $3 million. Technical support and consultation for long-term sustainability are also provided. Guidelines for the next round will be released in Spring 2026.
grants.eligibility_notes: Nonprofit arts organizations based in New York State with operating budgets under $3 million and prior NYSCA funding within the last three years.

key_requirements: Must be a nonprofit arts organization based in New York State
Operating budget under $3 million
Received NYSCA funding in the last three years
Commitment to establishing or building a reserve fund
